---
title: "Tools"
---

[Built with AI]{.eyebrow}

Software I design and build myself, with large language models as a coding partner. Each one
started as a real friction in my own days, a way to procrastinate less, reflect more, or make
sense of my own data, and grew into a working app I actually use. Same instinct as the lab
tools: find the thing that's missing, then build it.

::::: {.card-grid}

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" style="object-fit:cover;object-position:center" src="tools/focus-pocus/app-interface.png" alt="The Focus Pocus timer window with a running timer and controls">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [Focus Pocus](tools/focus-pocus/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[Desktop app · in development]{.paper-meta}

A focus timer that notices when you drift off, whether it's an idle mouse or a YouTube tab, and
beeps you back louder and louder until you return. Built to get my PhD thesis written.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard .is-soon}
<div class="pcard-ph">Coming soon</div>

::: {.pcard-body}
### Dear Diary
[Desktop app]{.paper-meta}

A private daily journal with a quick "simple" mode and a free-writing "chatty" mode, plus a
stats view that turns a month of entries into moods, sleep, and habits.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard .is-soon}
<div class="pcard-ph">Coming soon</div>

::: {.pcard-body}
### Gratitude Journal
[Progressive web app]{.paper-meta}

A lightweight gratitude practice with daily prompts, mood tracking, and streaks, that runs in the
browser and keeps everything on your own device.
:::

::::

:::::
